steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage uses 'However' to signal a contrast: the three-host CLP's abundance dropped with rising temperature while the directly transmitted parasite's abundance stayed stable. The correct choice must explain that contrast.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘CLPs primarily transmitted by ingestion were less dependent on host species adversely affected by warming temperatures than were CLPs that use other transmission strategies.’: Contradicts the data — CLPs' abundance did drop, indicating they were more dependent. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘any advantages that the transmission strategy used by three-host CLPs may have conferred did not completely offset the negative effects of other temperature-driven factors on CLP abundance.’: Matches the observed drop in CLP abundance despite any benefits of using multiple hosts. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘directly transmitted parasites identified in the study were more likely to use transmission strategies that shield them from warming temperatures than were three-host CLPs.’: The passage shows stable abundance for directly transmitted parasites, but this choice misattributes the mechanism in a way the data doesn't support. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘as the number of host species involved in a parasite's transmission increases, the parasite is better protected against rising temperatures.’: Conflicts with the data, which show the opposite — more hosts correlated with a drop, not protection. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
